label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this square dance problem. So there are five dancers: Alice, Bob, Claire, Dave, and Eve. Each of them starts with a partner: Alice with Lola, Bob with Karl, Claire with Patrick, Dave with Sam, and Eve with Helga. Then there are a series of partner swaps, and we need to determine who Bob is dancing with at the end. The options are Lola, Karl, Patrick, Sam, or Helga. First, I need to track each partner swap step by step. Let me jot down the initial pairings: Initial: - Alice → Lola - Bob → Karl - Claire → Patrick - Dave → Sam - Eve → Helga Now, the sequence of swaps is as follows: 1. Dave and Alice switch partners. 2. Eve and Alice switch partners. 3. Bob and Dave switch partners. 4. Bob and Eve switch partners. 5. Bob and Claire switch partners. Let me go through each step one by one. First swap: Dave and Alice switch partners. So whatever partners Dave and Alice had initially will be exchanged. Initially, Dave was with Sam, and Alice was with Lola. So after swapping, Dave will be with Lola, and Alice will be with Sam. So after first swap: - Alice → Sam - Dave → Lola Others remain the same: - Bob → Karl - Claire → Patrick - Eve → Helga Second swap: Eve and Alice switch partners. So Eve's partner is currently Helga, and Alice's partner is Sam. After swapping, Eve will be with
